LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANTS SALO RAWET, AND DAVID GELLMAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MATTER IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON JUL-31-2023 AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL LANDLORD-TENANT/REAL PROPERTY CASES PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY, IF ANY, TO DEPARTMENT 501 BY FRIDAY, JUL-21- 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. Off Calendar Jun-07-2024 Continued To Aug-07-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SALO RAWET AND DAVID GELLMAN's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Salo Rawet and David Gellman's demurrer to the second amended complaint ("SAC") is overruled.

In 2020, plaintiff Daniel Cohen allegedly discovered roots growing from defendants' property onto his own that required excavation and drainage to repair. Over the next few months, the neighbors allegedly entered into three oral contracts to build a trench and French drain on defendants' property. Disagreements arose, prompting Cohen to sue in 2023. Defendants now demur to the fourth and fifth causes of action in the SAC for (1) breach of oral and/or implied-in-fact contracts, and (2) breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.

First, defendants argue Cohen fails to allege consideration. To find consideration, (1) the promisee must confer or agree to confer a benefit, or suffer or agree to suffer prejudice, and (2) the benefit or prejudice must actually be bargained for as the exchange for the promise. (Civ. Code 1605; Steiner v. Thexton (2010) 48 Cal.4th 411, 420-421.) Here, Cohen, the alleged promisee, agreed to confer the benefit of installing a drain at no cost that would prevent future damage to defendants' property, as well as his own. (SAC para. 97, 98.) In exchange, Cohen received permission to build the drain on defendants' land. (Id.) These allegations suffice to show consideration for the second and third contracts (Cohen concedes the first is time-barred).

Second, defendants argue Cohen fails to show breach of the second oral contract. Cohen alleges defendants demanded a narrower French drain that would have rendered it inoperable, which suffices to show breach. (SAC para. 101.)

Third, defendants argue Cohen fails to show formation of the third contract because the defendants, especially Rawet, are never alleged to have assented to it. Cohen pleads that Gellman responded to his offer by stating it was practical and generous and offered defendants' enthusiastic support. (SAC para. 105.) Drawing reasonable inferences from these allegations, Cohen has adequately pled mutual assent to the third contract.

Fourth, defendants argue Cohen fails to allege performance of the third contract because Cohen never alleges he paid defendants $5,000 as promised. Cohen, however, alleges an excuse for nonperformance: defendants agreed to allow an 18" French drain to be built on their property but then reneged and did not allow Cohen to install it. (SAC para. 104, 107.) These allegations support an inference of material breach so as to excuse performance.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
